archetype_reasoning: The vast majority of resolved trades are concentrated in a single market (Trump/Judy Shelton Fed chair) with NO entries at 0.955-0.965 — classic near-certainty yield harvesting. The 98.1% win rate across 107 Trump_US trades is almost entirely explained by repeatedly selling near-zero probability outcomes rather than genuine forecasting. The one loss came from buying YES at 0.023 in the same market, suggesting occasional LP-style or error trades alongside the harvesting strategy.
domain_blindspots: Iran_ME: only 1 resolved trade, open positions show both YES and NO on same market (regime fall), suggesting uncertainty or LP behaviour rather than genuine edge
Trump_US (edge quality): high win rate is likely mechanical near-certainty harvesting, not deep fundamental forecasting
domain_summary: This trader has a mechanical, near-certainty harvesting edge in Trump_US by repeatedly fading low-probability outcomes. There is no demonstrated genuine forecasting edge outside this strategy, and Iran_ME positions are exploratory at best.
signal_weight_reasoning: Nearly all wins are generated by buying NO at 0.95+ in a single market, which is yield harvesting rather than genuine signal. The high ELO and win rate are artifacts of this strategy and not indicative of forecasting skill. Iran_ME positions are small, exploratory, and include contradictory directional bets (YES and NO on same outcome), providing no usable signal.
behavioural_flags: SEVERE_DECAY_2026-07-13: geo_elo_active collapsed from 2542.16 → 544.5. This is not a gradual recency decay — the score has fallen below Pool C functional levels. Oscar must review whether this is a data artifact or reflects a genuine abandonment of this wallet. Profile retained per Rule 7 but this address is effectively retired unless the geo_elo pipeline confirms an anomaly.

risk_patterns: Massive concentration risk: effectively a single-market strategy masking as diversified geo trading

Low realized PnL ($314) relative to 108 trades confirms thin-margin harvesting, not high-value forecasting

open_positions_summary: Open book is dominated by Iran_ME plays: a large YES on US-Iran ceasefire by March 31 (565 shares at 0.23), a significant NO on Iranian regime falling by June 30 (519 shares at 0.67), and small contradictory positions on Iranian regime fall before 2027 (both YES and NO). There are also near-zero speculative bets on US forces entering Iran and a Kevin Warsh Fed nomination play. The thesis implied is cautious Iran engagement — ceasefire likely, regime stable in near term — but the hedging on regime fall undermines confidence in any single directional read.
watch_items: Monitor whether ceasefire YES position (565 shares at 0.23) resolves — this is the largest genuine directional bet and will reveal if trader has real Iran_ME edge
Watch for continuation of Judy Shelton-style harvesting in new Fed nomination markets as chair question evolves
Track whether contradictory Iran regime positions are resolved by closing one leg — directional clarity would upgrade Iran_ME signal value
Kevin Warsh nomination position is a genuine contested-price call (0.27) — outcome will test Trump_US forecasting outside harvesting context
summary: This trader's impressive ELO and near-perfect win rate are almost entirely explained by a mechanical near-certainty yield harvesting strategy in a single market cluster (Judy Shelton Fed nomination NO at 0.955+), not genuine geopolitical forecasting. The $314 realized PnL across 108 trades confirms thin-margin premium collection rather than high-conviction calls. Open positions show exploratory Iran_ME interest with contradictory directional bets, suggesting the trader is transitioning or diversifying but has no demonstrated edge outside the harvesting niche. Signal weight should be minimal — the ELO tier is misleading and should not be used as a forecasting endorsement.
